title: Bear Encounter
immediate_steps: Identify the bear (Brown/Grizzly vs. Black bear)
If a Black Bear: stand your ground, make yourself look large, yell, wave your arms, and fight back aggressively if it attacks

If a Brown/Grizzly Bear: do not make eye contact, back away slowly, speak in a calm voice. If it charges, stand your ground (most are bluff charges). If it makes contact, play dead (lie on stomach, hands over neck, legs spread)
avoid: Do not run (bears are faster than horses, running triggers a predatory chase)
Do not climb a tree (black bears are excellent climbers, grizzlies can reach high)

title: Mountain Lion / Cougar Encounter
immediate_steps: Maintain intense eye contact with the animal
Make yourself look as large as possible (open your jacket, raise your arms)
Make loud noises and throw rocks or sticks if it approaches
If attacked, fight back with everything you have (aim for the eyes and nose)
avoid: Do not bend down or crouch (it makes you look like prey)
